subject: Bug fix in HELM charts for portal-app component
commit_message:
Bug fix in HELM charts for portal-app component

in the deployment.yaml, fixed the ownership of
/usr/local/tomcat/temp folder to portal:portal

Issue-ID: OOM-2557
Signed-off-by: SandeepLinux <Sandeep.Shah@att.com>
Change-Id: Idde371c25c0c4cd6b4fa4b4c08cc17ff9af517f2

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I'm sorry but your user shouldn't have "sudo" power as it's the same as being root...

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> I'm sorry but your user shouldn't have "sudo" power as it's the same as being root...

Hi Sylvain - Not sure how to address this...We do need to execute this command via deployment.yaml, when container comes up...Portal app container comes up as "portal" user, and portal's groups are ONLY "portal" as seen from the container itself: 

bash-4.4$ whoami
portal
bash-4.4$ groups portal
portal

Since all folders are owned by "root" as "root" is a default, when docker container comes up as "portal" we need to have access to "/usr/local/tomcat/temp", as this folder is owned by root...That is the only reason to change the ownership to portal:portal...

I tried to bring up portal container without "sudo" but it did not come up properly...For the benefit of time, I propose that we merge this; while in the meanwhile, we find a suitable solution...I would appreciate if you please advise.  Without this command, the below is the situation:

ash-4.4$ ls -lt /usr/local/tomcat
total 152
drwxrwxrwx    1 root     root          4096 Sep 14 19:40 work
drwxrwxrwx    2 root     root          4096 Sep 14 19:39 logs
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root          4096 Nov 16  2018 temp
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root         19539 Nov  3  2018 BUILDING.txt
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root          6090 Nov  3  2018 CONTRIBUTIN

Thank you!

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

There are 2 ways to handle this:

1) Mount /usr/local/tomcat/temp as an emptyDir volume with world RW rights
2) chown your /usr/local/tomcat/temp in your Dockerfile

Patch Set 1:

but in either cases you should remove sudo privileges from portal user

Abandoned

Modified Dockerfile...and helm update no more required.  https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/portal/+/112719
